Title: Identification and characterisation of serotonin signalling in the potato cyst nematode Globodera pallida reveals new targets for crop protection

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Vesicular monoamine transporter; major facilitator superfamily transporter
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports vesicular storage of serotonin/biogenic amines required for endogenous serotonin-dependent stylet thrusting and root invasion; target of reserpine
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotransmitter essential for stylet activation and root invasion behaviour
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Synaptic vesicles of serotonergic/biogenic amine neurons
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gpa-cat-1 was identified as the G. pallida orthologue of the C. elegans vesicular monoamine transporter cat-1 and cloned as two likely allelic variants. 2. Expression of gpa-cat-1 in C. elegans cat-1(ok411) partially rescued defective pharyngeal pumping and restored fluoxetine sensitivity. 3. Pharmacology with reserpine showed that depletion of endogenous vesicular serotonin blocks fluoxetine-induced but not exogenous serotonin-induced stylet thrusting in G. pallida. 4. Reserpine treatment reduced nematode dispersal and strongly impaired root invasion, linking CAT-1-dependent monoamine storage to parasitic behaviour.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cross-species complementation in C. elegans cat-1 mutant; fluoxetine response restoration; reserpine pharmacology; stylet thrusting assay; dispersal assay; root invasion assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly reported; inhibition suppresses movement and invasion
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CAT-1 transports multiple biogenic amines, so some behavioural effects of reserpine on dispersal may involve transmitters in addition to serotonin.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Validated molecular target of reserpine and promising target for crop-protective compounds that deplete nematode monoamine signaling.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Psnb-1 (pan-neuronal, in C. elegans complementation assays)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from G. pallida draft genome; J2 cDNA cloning

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tryptophan hydroxylase; aromatic amino acid hydroxylase family
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Serotonin production branch of biogenic amine metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates L-tryptophan to 5-hydroxy-L-tryptophan, the rate-setting first step of serotonin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for serotonin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotransmitter required for stylet thrusting, root invasion and parasitic behaviours in G. pallida
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endogenous serotonin synthesis required for fluoxetine-induced stylet thrusting and efficient root invasion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gpa-tph-1 was identified as the G. pallida orthologue of C. elegans tph-1 and cloned from J2 cDNA. 2. Expression of gpa-tph-1 rescued the reduced pharyngeal pumping phenotype of C. elegans tph-1(mg280), supporting conserved tryptophan hydroxylase function. 3. The TPH inhibitor CPA phenocopied tph-1 loss in C. elegans and its effect was restored in tph-1 mutants expressing gpa-tph-1. 4. In G. pallida, CPA blocked fluoxetine-induced but not serotonin-induced stylet thrusting and reduced root invasion, indicating TPH-1-dependent serotonin synthesis is necessary for parasitic behaviour.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key upstream control step for serotonin production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cross-species complementation in C. elegans tph-1 mutant; pharmacological inhibition with CPA; stylet thrusting assay; root invasion assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly reported; inhibition suppresses parasitic behaviour
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RNAi was avoided because soaking dsRNA caused nonspecific toxicity to G. pallida stylet thrusting, so pharmacology was used instead.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TPH-1 is a candidate target for nematode control chemicals that suppress serotonin-dependent invasion behaviours.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Psnb-1 (pan-neuronal, in C. elegans complementation assays)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from G. pallida draft genome; transcript support from J2 cDNA

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Serotonin signal transduction controlling stylet thrusting and feeding-related rhythmic motor output
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Neural/pharyngeal signaling circuits controlling pumping and stylet activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates serotonin signaling required for serotonin-triggered motor behaviours linked to parasitism; pharmacological blockade inhibits stylet thrusting and root invasion
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-affinity serotonin receptor identified as a key determinant of serotonin-dependent behavioural output
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotransmitter required for stylet thrusting, root invasion and parasitic behaviour
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gpa-ser-7 was identified as the closest G. pallida orthologue of C. elegans ser-7 and cloned. 2. Expression of gpa-ser-7 rescued serotonin-stimulated pharyngeal pumping defects of C. elegans ser-7(tm1325). 3. In cut-head assays, serotonin activated pumping in transgenics expressing gpa-ser-7, and methiothepin antagonized this response. 4. Methiothepin potently blocked G. pallida stylet thrusting and root invasion, supporting SER-7 as a likely actionable target in parasitism.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cross-species complementation in C. elegans ser-7 mutant; cut-head pharmacology; serotonin dose-response; methiothepin antagonism; G. pallida stylet thrusting and root invasion assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly reported; receptor antagonism blocks hatching/root invasion behaviours
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes SER-7 is not the sole receptor contributing to serotonin response in intact nematodes; residual response remains in C. elegans ser-7 mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising nematicide target because receptor antagonists can impair invasion-related behaviour.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Psnb-1 (pan-neuronal, in C. elegans complementation assays)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from G. pallida predicted gene complement

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Serotonin-gated chloride channel; ligand-gated ion channel
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Serotonin signal transduction controlling locomotion, paralysis-related responses, stylet thrusting and hatching-associated behaviour
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Neural circuits responding directly to serotonin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates serotonin-dependent behavioural responses; pharmacological blockade is associated with inhibition of stylet thrusting, root invasion and hatching
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Invertebrate-specific serotonin receptor/channel and strong candidate target for serotonin-dependent parasitic behaviours
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotransmitter required for behavioural programs supporting parasitism
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gpa-mod-1 was identified and cloned as a G. pallida orthologue of the invertebrate-specific serotonin-gated chloride channel MOD-1. 2. Expression of gpa-mod-1 rescued the serotonin-induced paralysis phenotype of C. elegans mod-1(ok103), confirming functional orthology. 3. Methiothepin blocked MOD-1-dependent serotonin responses in both wild-type C. elegans and transgenics expressing gpa-mod-1. 4. Because methiothepin also potently blocked G. pallida stylet thrusting, root invasion and hatching, MOD-1 is implicated as an important target in parasitic behaviour.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cross-species complementation in C. elegans mod-1 mutant; serotonin-induced paralysis assay; methiothepin antagonism; G. pallida behaviour assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Serotonin exposure; methiothepin treatment; potato root diffusate in hatching assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly reported; antagonism inhibits hatching and invasion behaviours
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MOD-1 is highlighted as invertebrate-specific, making it attractive for selective crop protection strategies with potentially reduced off-target toxicity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value selective nematicide target because MOD-1 is absent from vertebrates and linked to hatching/invasion behaviours.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Pmod-1 (native mod-1 promoter, in C. elegans complementation assays)
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining from G. pallida predicted gene complement
